message:
fix(tui): harden focus ownership and Enter-routing across input modes

Resolves a class of bugs where pressing Enter after interacting with
result rows via mouse would apply a stale filter input instead of opening
the detail modal. The root cause was that mouse interactions in the
results pane did not update the focus manager, so the input-mode router
still believed the search bar owned keyboard input.

Key changes:
- New enter_results_navigation_context() method transitions focus to
  RESULTS_LIST and resets input mode to Query when mouse interactions
  occur in the results pane (clicks, scrolls)
- Input-mode routing for non-Query modes now gates on focus being on
  SEARCH_BAR, so Enter with results-list focus always opens detail
  regardless of the last input mode
- SelectionMoved, SelectionJumped, and ActivePaneChanged messages
  transfer focus to RESULTS_LIST when in Query mode
- InputModeEntered and PaneFilterChanged correctly set focus to
  SEARCH_BAR so filter editing is not disrupted
- Filter pill clicks (agent, ws, pane, time) route focus to SEARCH_BAR

Test coverage added:
- mouse_click_in_results_restores_results_navigation_context
- enter_after_mouse_result_click_opens_detail_from_stale_input_mode
- Focus ownership suite: selection_moved, selection_jumped,
  active_pane_changed, keyboard navigation → detail, detail close
- Enter-routing matrix: query mode ± hit, results focus, re-entrant
  detail, pane filter mode, agent filter mode, full roundtrip flow

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
